ddls:
CREATE TABLE "product_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the product belongs to */,
  "fabric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fabric used for the product */,
  "production_batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production batch the product belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the product */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the product image */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock-keeping unit (SKU) for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_charts" (
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size chart */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the size chart belongs to */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size */,
  "body_length" TEXT /* Body length of the product */,
  "body_width" TEXT /* Body width of the product */,
  "full_body_length" TEXT /* Full body length of the product */,
  "sleeve_length" TEXT /* Sleeve length of the product */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Measurement unit (e.g., inches, cm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_chart_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size */,
  "size_name" TEXT /* Name of the size (e.g., S, M, L, XL) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the size */,
  "size_order" INTEGER /* Order of the size (e.g., 1 for S, 2 for M, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement parameter */,
  "measurement_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement parameter (e.g., body length, body width) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement parameter */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Measurement unit (e.g., inches, cm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_measurements" (
  "product_measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the measurement belongs to */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the measurement */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Measurement unit (e.g., inches, cm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fabric_types" (
  "fabric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fabric type */,
  "fabric_name" TEXT /* Name of the fabric type (e.g., Cotton, Denim) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the fabric type */,
  "composition" TEXT /* Fabric composition (e.g., 100% cotton) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fabric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_batches" (
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production batch */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the batch belongs to */,
  "batch_number" TEXT /* Production batch number */,
  "production_date" TEXT /* Date of production */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity produced */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., designer, product manager, customer service) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., product, size chart) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sale_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sale */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product sold */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date of sale */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity sold */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* Revenue generated from the sale */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sale_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_reconciliations" (
  "reconciliation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size reconciliation */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the reconciliation belongs to */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size */,
  "reconciliation_date" TEXT /* Date of reconciliation */,
  "discrepancy_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the discrepancy (e.g., measurement error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reconciliation_id")
)
